description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 36-39.

Legendary Cleric

Clerics are known far and wide as vessels of a higher power, chosen ones of varying might who speak with a voice and authority that is not their own–much as they might agree with it. Where Legendary Clerics are concerned, that line begins to blur until it disappears entirely. Their capacity of channeling a Divine Domain is such that they come to incarnate it, partially or wholly becoming the deities they once looked up to in devotion and prayer. One must accept that the right hand of the God of Light, for example, is an extension of the God of Light. At the highest echelon of achievement, devotion begets fusion with its object, and ultimately… apotheosis.

A Legendary Cleric may refuse this interpretation of their newfound power, instead pointing to extraordinary divine favor granted for equally extraordinary devotion. The fact remains that, at this tier, their faith has become indistinguishable from divine authority. Beyond 20th level, a Cleric does not petition; they speak directly on behalf of Divinity. Their magic is rooted in declarations, issued by someone who has spent a lifetime becoming the closest thing to a God that a mortal can manage without ceasing to be a mortal. Armies halt at their word. The dead return at their beckoning. The planes themselves respond to their sacred presence.

In the midst of other Legendary Characters, a Legendary Cleric stands out as an unparalleled beacon of legitimacy. Divinity, at its core, is the foundation of the Universe, and those who incarnate it are its architects and keepers, be they Gods… or clerics.

3 6 5 25 4 3 3 3 3 3 3 2 1 1

Divine Indemnity

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Cleric table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or

Cantrips

Prepared

Known

Spells

magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Burst of Divinity

Also at 20th level, as an action, you can expend 1 Legendary Point and point your holy symbol at a creature you can see within 60 feet. You either restore hit points to the creature equal to your level x 5 or have the creature take an amount of Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice) equal to your level x 5.

Level 21

Heroic Path Advancement

At 21st level, you choose a Heroic Path you meet the prerequisites for and advance its first step. Each time you gain a level, you can advance a Heroic Path by one step, start a new Heroic Path, or, if specified as an option of the current step you’re on, jump from one Heroic Path into another.

Legendary Score Improvement

When you reach 21st level, and again at levels 23 and 25, increase one of your ability scores by 2 or two ability scores by 1. You cannot raise an ability score above 24 using this feature, unless another feature raised your maximum for that specific ability score beforehand.

Warded by a Higher Power

Also at 21st level, your God ensures your protection if you are ever in true danger. Whenever you take damage or are subjected to an effect that would reduce you to 0 hit points or kill you outright, you can use your reaction to regain 50 hit points and become immune to all damage for one minute. This protection affects the triggering damage. If you make an attack, use an object, or cast a spell, this protection ends.

Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

Level 22

10th-Level Spell

At 22nd level, you learn a single 10th-level spell. You learn an additional 10th-level spell at 24th level. When you start casting a 10th-level spell, you must expend 2 Legendary Points alongside the required action and spell slot.

Divine Mandate

Also at 22nd level, the sacred ordainment that guides you becomes powerful enough to momentarily warp reality to your God’s will, needing only the right words. As an action on your turn, you can cause one of the following effects. You must be able to speak to use this ability.

To Dust Commend Thy Walls. Choose a creature you can see within 60 feet, and select either two damage types it is resistant to, or one damage type it is immune to. It loses those resistances or that immunity for the next minute.

Weight Be Upon The Sinful. Creatures of your choice within 60 feet must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be knocked prone and be incapacitated until the start of your next turn. Creatures whose alignment is opposite to yours in any axis roll this saving throw with disadvantage.

Dauntless Are The Virtuous. Creatures of your choice within 60 feet roll all ability checks, attack rolls, contests, and saving throws with advantage until the start of your next turn.

Level 23

Heraldic Favor

Beginning at 23rd level, you become the foremost herald of your God. Your voice is theirs, and you can

perform miracles in their name. After successfully performing a Divine Intervention, you can do so again after you complete a long rest, instead of having to wait 7 days. In addition, your Divine Intervention becomes even more powerful, able to replicate any spell of 9th level or lower from the Cleric spell list.

Level 24

Fountain of Grace

Beginning at 24th level, your holy presence constantly empowers you and your allies. Creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you have a +2 bonus to ability checks, attack rolls, contests, and saving throws.

Blessed Recovery

Also at 24th level, when you use the Recover action, you regain all uses of your Channel Divinity feature.

Level 25

Divine Indemnity

At 25th level, your God grants you control over the powers of protection they have bestowed upon your blessed self.

You can expend 2 Legendary Points and use your action to give a creature within 60 feet of you immunity to all damage for up to 1 minute. You can only protect one creature in this manner at a time. The protection breaks if the target creature is ever farther than 500 feet away from you or on a different plane of existence. This counts as a magical effect of 9th level for the purposes of Tiers of Magic and uses your concentration.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
